sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund the Cardano Builder DAO as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism for Cardano builders aligned with Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with funds initially received and administered by an independent council/multisig composed of dRep DAO members before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL., Operate proposal review, feedback coordination, transparent fund distribution, and KPI/result tracking for funded work., Continue KPI dashboard development toward live on-chain Cardano data tracking and recurring KPI reporting standards with dReps., Disburse funds only after milestone completion is validated and confirmed by the administrator.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, included 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, and achieved 83% governance participation.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 KPI dashboards, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
missing: Independent evidence for: The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, included 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, and achieved 83% governance participation.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 KPI dashboards, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
